pi_items.source: Verified through setup_task.sh (line 11-13), export_result.sh (line 15, 20, 24), and RFC 6761 (.test TLD is reserved for testing)
Standard X.509 Distinguished Name field; verified through openssl certificate generation with identical field. Valid org name for X.509 certificates.
Standard X.509 Distinguished Name field (Organizational Unit); verified through openssl certificate generation with identical field. Valid org unit for X.509 certificates.
Standard X.509 Distinguished Name field (Locality); verified through openssl certificate generation. San Francisco is a valid US city.
Standard X.509 Distinguished Name field (State/Province); verified through openssl certificate generation. California is a valid US state.
Standard X.509 Distinguished Name field (Country Code); verified through openssl certificate generation. US is a valid ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code.
Verified through openssl certificate generation test: generated 365-day cert (notBefore: Mar 8 2026, notAfter: Mar 8 2027). Verifier accepts range 335-395 days (verifier.py:121).

Verified in setup_task.sh line 26 (removed to start clean) and export_result.sh lines 54-74 (checked as first candidate path). /home/acmecorp is the standard home directory for the acmecorp domain user in Virtualmin.
Verified in setup_task.sh line 26 (removed to start clean). Corresponds to private key location alongside the certificate file.

privileged_info_summary: The task 'enable_ssl_website' is a synthetic test environment task for Virtualmin (a web hosting control panel). It requires configuring SSL for the domain 'acmecorp.test' (a reserved test domain) and generating a self-signed X.509 certificate with specific organizational details: Organization=Acme Corporation, Organizational Unit=Web Services, Locality=San Francisco, State=California, Country=US, with a validity period of 365 days. The certificate files are expected at /home/acmecorp/ssl.cert (public cert) and /home/acmecorp/ssl.key (private key). All metadata claims regarding domain name, certificate fields (O, OU, L, ST, C), and validity period have been verified as correct X.509 specifications and standard certificate validity periods.
